summary: Astronomical observation catalog from Pachacamac — the 3rd confirmed observatory site in the ALBA network. 90 cords, 9 clusters (= 9-column format), 19 STRING (100% hits). 7/10 ALBA celestial labels: kaki (Pleiades x4), chaki (Scorpio x2, sv=140 PEAK), kama (Mars x2), taki (Saturn? x2), mama (Moon x1), qaqa (dark const. x1), maki (Mars obs x1). Highest label density per STRING cord in corpus. Two-color text split: B (brown = opening) vs LC (lilac = RITUAL observations, 58% of text). DATING: Mode C C13=43 → ~1481 CE (working hypothesis, MEDIUM confidence); total 18,043d = 49.4yr; Mars ephemeris tested but NON-CONCLUSIVE (hits point to different start dates, no single convergence). Museum für Völkerkunde, Berlin.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  90 cords. 19 STRING (21%, 100% hits), 56 INT, 15 EMPTY (17%).
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, depth L2. 9 clusters = 9-column astronomical format.
  4 colors: DB(45 = 50%), LC(24 = 27%), B(20 = 22%), BB(1).
  Provenance: Pachacamac. Museum: Museum für Völkerkunde, Berlin.
  OKR: Cord 37 broken at 16cm + pendant fragment.

7/10 ASTRONOMICAL LABELS
  kaki (Pleiades) x4 — B(2) + LC(2) = crosses both colors
  chaki (Scorpio) x2 — LC, sv=0 and sv=140 (PEAK)
  kama (Mars events) x2 — LC
  taki (Saturn?/ritual) x2 — LC
  mama (Moon) x1 — LC
  qaqa (dark constellations) x1 — B
  maki (Mars observations) x1 — LC
  Plus: pama (formula x1), tama (herd x1), paki (break x1), chama (food x1, sv=10)
  Highest label density: 7 labels in 19 STRING = 37% celestial names.

COLOR-AS-ROLE — RITUAL ASTRONOMY
  DB (50%): NUMERICAL DATA — observation counts
  LC (lilac, 27%): RITUAL/ASTRONOMICAL LABELS — 11/19 STRING = 58% of text is RITUAL
  B (brown, 22%): OPENING/GEOGRAPHIC — initial labels (pama, tama, qaqa, kaki)
  The LC color proves celestial observation was encoded as RITUAL, not secular.

OBSERVATORY NETWORK (4 sites)
  Leymebamba (UR006, 1473): 10/10 labels, 24 months
  Arica (AS069/AS070, 1453): 6-7/10, 30 years
  Pachacamac (UR1145, THIS): 7/10, catalog
  Incahuasi (UR278, ~1350-1512): 1/10, 162 years

NARRATIVE — THE RITUAL CATALOG OF PACHACAMAC
  Formula. That is the opening word — *pama*, cord 6, on brown thread. The catalog begins with the ritual words that consecrate the observation. Then the herd (*tama*) — because the dark cloud constellations are named after animals. The rock (*qaqa*, cord 11) names the dark constellations themselves.
  The Pleiades dominate: *kaki* four times, crossing both colors — twice on brown (the catalog entry), twice on lilac (the ritual observation). Their heliacal rising signaled the planting season; at Pachacamac, the greatest oracle, knowing when the Pleiades would rise was knowing when the god would speak about the year.
  Then the lilac cords take over. Cord 51: *chaki* — Scorpio, the Foot-Plough. Cord 53: *kama* — Mars. Cord 55: *chaki* again, with a prefix of 140 — the highest value in the khipu. One hundred and forty observations of Scorpio, the primary target.
  Near the end: *mama* (cord 83) — the Moon. *Taki* (cord 85) — the observation chant, or Saturn. All on lilac — the color of ritual. The astronomer at Pachacamac observed the stars as a priest observes the sacred: with ceremony, with formula, with the colors that mark the boundary between data and devotion.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Astronomical catalog, observatory network, LC=ritual astronomy
Urton 1981, At the Crossroads of the Earth and the Sky — Andean constellations
